◈ epost_agent_kit
Agents CLI Workflows Install GitHub
All Workflows
Ideation → Validation

Brainstorming Flow

From vague idea to validated spike. Agents generate options, evaluate trade-offs, design the UI direction, build a proof-of-concept, and review viability.

epost-brainstormer epost-planner epost-muji epost-fullstack-developer epost-code-reviewer

Five Phases

1

Ideate

epost-brainstormer

Generate 5–8 solution approaches with trade-offs

  • · Divergent thinking — no idea rejected in this phase
  • · Each option gets: summary, pros, cons, effort estimate
  • · Cross-platform considerations surfaced early
  • · Prior art checked via knowledge-retrieval
2

Evaluate

epost-planner

Score options on feasibility, effort, impact, risk

  • · Decision matrix with weighted criteria
  • · Technical feasibility against current architecture
  • · Dependency analysis — what needs to exist first
  • · Top 2 options selected for spike
3

Design

epost-muji

Sketch UI direction for selected approach

  • · Component breakdown from design system
  • · Information architecture and user flow
  • · Existing pattern reuse identified
  • · Figma-to-code mapping prepared
4

Spike

epost-fullstack-developer

Build a minimal proof-of-concept on a spike branch

  • · Smallest possible vertical slice
  • · Working UI connected to stub data
  • · Validates technical assumptions from Evaluate
  • · Branch: spike/[feature-name]
5

Validate

epost-code-reviewer

Review spike for viability and flag blockers

  • · Does it prove the concept?
  • · Are there architectural red flags?
  • · Performance characteristics acceptable?
  • · Recommendation: proceed, pivot, or drop

Example Scenarios

"We need a way for users to collaborate on documents"

Brainstormer generates:
1. Real-time CRDT editor (Yjs)
2. Comment threads with @mentions
3. Version history with diff view
4. Shared workspace with cursors
Planner selects: Comment threads — lowest complexity, highest adoption likelihood

"The mobile app feels slow on the inbox screen"

Brainstormer generates:
1. Virtualized list with recycling
2. Server-side pagination refactor
3. Offline-first with sync queue
4. Skeleton loading with prefetch
Planner selects: Virtualized list — addresses root cause, no API changes needed
◈

Ideas Are Cheap. Validated Spikes Aren't.

The brainstorming flow doesn't stop at ideas — it pushes through to a working proof-of-concept that proves or disproves the approach. By the time a human reviews the result, the question isn't "should we try this?" but "does this spike validate our assumptions?" Faster decisions, less wasted effort.

◈ epost_agent_kit v2.0.0
Works with any AI coding assistant
